name: Anand Bhagat
body: Anand leads the practice with a singular conviction: that architecture should be discovered, not imposed. For four decades, he has worked across every scale — from intimate private homes to landmark corporate campuses — bringing the same quality of thought to each. He remains the primary design authority on every commission the studio takes on.

name: Amala Sheth
body: Amala co-founded Metaphors on the belief that architecture is an act of revealing the symbiotic relationship between form and function. Her philosophy — rooted in rhythm, materiality, and cultural context — established the studio's refusal to adopt a house style. Her intellectual rigour remains the bedrock of every brief we deliver.

name: Vanita Mali
body: Vanita oversees the high-end residential and resort portfolio. Her approach is meticulous and end-to-end — from the first spatial concept to the final fixture detail.

name: Manoj Rathod
body: Manoj leads commercial, institutional, and master-planning commissions. He brings structural rigour to complex briefs without sacrificing spatial intelligence.

name: Pauravi Mahajan-Pawar
body: Pauravi runs the interior architecture division — material-driven, tactile, and deeply integrated with the base build rather than layered on top.

name: Kanaka Bhosale
body: Kanaka focuses on corporate and institutional interiors, bringing a deep understanding of space planning for productivity and wellbeing.

name: Mayur Kamble
body: Mayur leads design visualization and concept rendering, bridging the gap between design thought and client communication through high-fidelity visuals.
